prompt:
Need a bounded in-memory cache for the thumbnail service — we're currently using a plain map and it grows until the pod OOMs. Please build package lru (root of module go-lru-cache), generic, stdlib only.

API:
- New[K comparable, V any](capacity int, onEvict func(K, V)) (*Cache[K, V], error) — error on capacity < 1; onEvict may be nil.
- Put(key K, value V), Get(key K) (V, bool), Remove(key K) bool, Len() int.

Semantics (this is where the last homegrown attempt went wrong, so being picky):
- strict LRU: when a Put would exceed capacity, drop the least recently USED entry — Get counts as use, and so does overwriting via Put. Insertion-order eviction is exactly the bug we're replacing.
- onEvict fires only for capacity evictions, synchronously, with the displaced key and its value. It must NOT fire for overwrites (Put on an existing key just replaces the value in place) nor for explicit Remove.
- Get miss returns the zero value and false; Remove returns whether the key was present; removed slots are reusable.
- The whole thing must be safe for concurrent use — thumbnail workers hit it from dozens of goroutines. Under load nothing may leak or double-count: every entry ever Put is either still resident or was reported to onEvict exactly once.

Acceptance tests are in lru_test.go (they instantiate Cache[string,int] and Cache[int,string]); leave that file alone. Ship when go test -race -timeout 30s ./... passes.
